note: Non-schema client struct layouts, reverse-engineered from client.dll. Offsets drift between builds.

structs.desc: Client input singleton — turns raw mouse/keyboard state into the per-tick CUserCmd sent to the server. Not a schema (networked) class.

fields.name: m_angViewAngles
fields.note: current command view angles — pitch 0x688 / yaw 0x68C / roll 0x690; mouse delta is added into yaw here each frame

fields.name: m_mouseAccumHistory
fields.note: input-delta smoothing sub-object {int count; void* samples; ...}

fields.name: m_pUserCmds
fields.note: ring buffer base, 1088-byte entries (add 0x18*slot for splitscreen)

structs.instance: (CCSGOInput*)(client.dll + 0x23B95F0) — static object embedded at this RVA (no deref; the GetInput getter returns its address)
structs.name: CCSGOInput

structs.desc: One entry in CCSGOInput's per-command ring buffer (1088 bytes / 0x440 each) — the resolved view angle + button masks CreateMove writes each tick. This is the client working buffer, not the networked protobuf.

fields.name: m_nButtonsQueuedChange
fields.note: bits that CHANGED this cmd (subtick gate)

fields.name: m_angViewAngles
fields.note: clamped view angle for this cmd — pitch 0x18 / yaw 0x1C / roll 0x20

structs.name: CUserCmd

structs.desc: The camera/view description filled each frame (fov, world origin, view angles). Written by OverrideView; read by the renderer. Not a schema class.

fields.name: m_vecOrigin
fields.note: world eye origin — x 0x4A0 / y 0x4A4 / z 0x4A8

structs.name: CViewSetup
